title: Crunchy Algerian Shrimp Tagine
description: A comforting, keto‑friendly shrimp tagine packed with oregano‑infused broth and a crunchy almond topping, perfect for a sick‑day meal.

ingredients.name: shrimp, peeled and deveined
ingredients.notes: fresh or frozen, thawed if frozen

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a medium‑sized tagine or heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and chopped onion, sautéing until the onion becomes translucent, about 3–4 minutes.
Stir in the diced tomatoes, oregano, cumin, and smoked paprika; cook for another 2 minutes until the tomatoes soften.
Pour in the chicken stock, bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, and season with salt and pepper.
Add the shrimp to the pot, spoon some broth over them, and reduce the heat to low.
Cover and let the shrimp poach in the simmering broth for 6–8 minutes, or until they turn opaque and pink.
Stir in the lemon juice, taste, and adjust seasoning if necessary.
Transfer the shrimp and sauce to a serving dish, then sprinkle the toasted almonds evenly over the top for a crunchy finish.
Serve immediately, optionally garnishing with a few fresh oregano leaves for added aroma.
